title: checked_mul for size multipliers is correct overflow handling
description: The lexer uses base.checked_mul(multiplier) when applying K/M/G suffixes to numeric literals (lexer.rs:189). A reviewer might say this is unnecessary since K/M/G are small constants and 'overflow can't happen in practice'. But the base value is u64-parsed from user input with no bounds check, so a script containing '9999999999999999999G' would overflow silently. The checked_mul with a proper ParseError is the correct defensive choice. Do not remove it.
